goal: Plan this as exactly two sprints. Add characterization tests that pin the CURRENT behavior of Voice Tutor, changing NO runtime behavior. Sprint 1: add pytest tests under `tests/` that characterize `documents.py` (title derivation, upload-validation error paths, text extraction — including the PDF path via the committed `tests/fixtures/sample.pdf` — and list/load), monkeypatching the documents directory to a temp path; change NO production source. Sprint 2: mechanically relocate the pure, Pipecat-free helper functions from `bot.py` ONLY into a new `session_state.py` that `bot.py` re-imports (a verbatim move — zero logic changes; any logic change inside a moved function is a failure), then add characterization tests for those helpers. Do NOT touch `wiki.py`, the STT/TTS/Pipecat pipeline, or the LLM-backed recap functions.
title: voice tutor characterization

sprints.title: characterize documents.py
sprints.description: Add pytest characterization tests under tests/ that pin the CURRENT behavior of documents.py, changing NO production source. Monkeypatch the documents directory to a temporary path so tests are hermetic. Cover: title derivation logic; upload-validation error paths (invalid types, oversized/empty inputs, and any other rejection branches as they currently behave); text extraction for all supported formats, INCLUDING the PDF path exercised via the committed tests/fixtures/sample.pdf fixture; and the list/load functions (listing stored documents and loading their contents/metadata). Tests must assert the observed present-day outputs and error types/messages verbatim — they document behavior, not correctness. Ensure pytest is runnable (add tests/__init__.py or minimal conftest.py only if needed for discovery/fixtures; do not alter production code). This sprint is a self-contained, shippable slice: documents.py behavior is now pinned by passing tests.

sprints.title: relocate and characterize bot helpers
sprints.description: Mechanically extract the pure, Pipecat-free helper functions from bot.py ONLY into a new session_state.py — a VERBATIM move with zero logic changes (any edit to the body of a moved function is a failure). bot.py then re-imports these helpers from session_state.py so all existing call sites and the public surface remain identical. Do NOT touch wiki.py, the STT/TTS/Pipecat pipeline, or the LLM-backed recap functions — only the pure session-state helpers move. After the move, add pytest characterization tests under tests/ that pin the current behavior of the relocated helpers (input/output for representative and edge-case inputs, exactly as they behave today). Verify by importing via both bot.py (re-export) and session_state.py to prove the move preserved the interface. This sprint is a self-contained, shippable slice: the refactor is behavior-preserving and now guarded by passing tests.
